results.rendered_html:
ā-√cam -cāmati ( Pāṇ. vii, 3, 75 ) to sip (water) from the palm of the hand for purification (with instr.  Mn. ii, 61 ),  ŚBr. ;  TBr.  &c.;
(perf. 3. p. -cemuḥ) to lap up, lick up, absorb, cause to disappear (as the winds lick up moisture,  Ragh. ix, 68 ; xiii, 20 ) :
Caus. (ind.p. -camayya) to cause to sip (water) for purification,  ŚāṅkhGṛ. ;
(Pot. -cāmayet; p. -cāmayat)
to cause to sip water,  Mn.

results.rendered_html:
ācam 1 P.
1 To sip, lick, lap drink (a small quantity); nāceme himamapi vāri vāraṇena  Ki. 7. 34 ; paryāptamācāmati  U. 4. 1 ;  Bv. 4. 38 ;  Ms. 2. 60 , 5. 139. 
2 To rinse the mouth (with instr. of the thing);  Ms. 2. 61. 
3 To lick up, dry or drink up, absorb; ācāmati svedalavān mukhe te  R. 13. 20. 
— Caus. To cause to sip water;  Ms. 3. 251 , 5. 142.
